outcome_contract.why: The current Brikette CI and deploy flow is harder to reason about than it should be. The operator wants one straightforward release model: changed-only validation on dev, staging as the preview lane reached by merge, and main as the live lane reached by merge.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Brikette has an analysis-ready fact-find for a simplified GitHub Actions setup where dev performs changed-only lint, typecheck, and tests in CI, staging is a merge-only preview lane, and main is a merge-only production lane.

engineering_coverage.applicable: No direct impact; leaves preview/live publication surface split across files
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No direct impact; preview/live lanes become one clearer publish surface
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No UI changes; simplify workflow ownership only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Operator has to remember which branch hits which workflow file
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Branch meaning becomes straightforward: `dev` validates, `staging` previews, `main` publishes live
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Prefer branch-to-workflow clarity over preserving current file layout

engineering_coverage.applicable: Manual dispatch remains tempting unless explicitly removed in multiple places
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Branch-only publish path narrows privileged execution paths
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Remove `workflow_dispatch` and keep credentialed deploys on branch merges only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Audit trail remains split across validation, staging, and production files
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: One validation workflow and one publish workflow make CI outcomes easier to audit
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Preserve branch-driven auditability while reducing workflow sprawl

engineering_coverage.applicable: Dev still has overlapping repo CI and Brikette CI concerns
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Dev validation centralizes on changed-scope CI; staging becomes first export proof point
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Remove Brikette dev smoke/export gates and keep export proof in staging/main publish flow

engineering_coverage.applicable: Reusable workflow inputs still obscure Brikette's actual deploy contract
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Branch/path triggers and explicit build/deploy steps become the contract
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Prefer explicit Brikette deploy steps over reusable-app indirection

engineering_coverage.applicable: Duplicate export/build logic and extra workflow fan-out remain
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Fewer workflow surfaces and no publish on irrelevant merges reduce wasted work
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Consolidate publish logic and keep path gates on `staging` and `main`

engineering_coverage.applicable: Promotion scripts still work, but lane responsibilities remain fuzzy
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Promotion scripts align directly to two workflow surfaces and symmetric publish rules
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Preserve current branch flow and make rollback/promotion reasoning simpler

stage_payload.decision_summary: Choose the simplest GitHub Actions shape for Brikette now that the operator has resolved the main policy forks: changed-scope validation in CI, merge-only staging and production publishes, no manual publish entrypoints, no publish when nothing Brikette-relevant changed, and no Brikette-specific smoke/static-export gating on `dev`.
goals: Make `dev` validation easy to reason about: changed-scope lint, typecheck, and tests in CI only.
Make `staging` the preview lane for humans and agents, entered by merge only.
Make `main` the live lane, entered by merge only.
Remove overlapping Brikette workflow responsibility and manual publish paths.
non_goals: Re-architect Brikette's static export shape or Cloudflare Pages footprint.
Solve every repo-wide CI governance concern unrelated to Brikette release flow.
Introduce bespoke file-level classifiers beyond what the push diff and its affected surface require.
constraints: Changed scope is defined by what is in the push and its affected dependency/workspace surface, with lint, typecheck, and tests run only where relevant.
Brikette-specific smoke/static-export checks are deferred from `dev`; the first export proof point moves to merge-driven staging.
`staging` and `main` are merge-only publish lanes with no `workflow_dispatch` fallback.
`staging` and `main` should skip publication when no Brikette-relevant paths changed.
assumptions: The current Pages staging host is sufficient for MCP and Playwright usage.
Consolidating workflows is preferable to preserving reusable workflow abstractions if the abstraction is part of the current complexity.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: The release model should be obvious from branch to workflow
CI should do only relevant lint/typecheck/test work
Fewer overlapping files and fewer special cases reduce maintenance
Staging and production should not rebuild/deploy on irrelevant merges
Removing dev smoke/export must still leave a clear export proof point before live

options_considered.Description: Keep the current split surfaces and trim obvious waste: retain `ci.yml`, keep a reduced `brikette.yml`, and keep `brikette-staging-fast.yml`
options_considered.Downside: Operator still has to reason across three overlapping workflow surfaces; Brikette remains partly special-cased
options_considered.Key risks: Complexity remains distributed; duplicate export logic persists

options_considered.Description: Collapse Brikette to two obvious workflow surfaces: `ci.yml` owns changed-scope dev validation, and one Brikette publish workflow handles merge-only staging and production with path-gated branch pushes
options_considered.Upside: Clearest mental model; removes manual publish paths; keeps staging/main behavior symmetrical; reduces Brikette-specific workflow count
options_considered.Downside: Requires refactoring current `brikette.yml` and likely retiring the reusable-app path for Brikette deploys
options_considered.Key risks: Need to preserve required production-only checks explicitly when leaving reusable-app

options_considered.Description: Build a bespoke Brikette-only validation workflow with custom per-file relevance logic, plus separate staging and production deploy workflows
options_considered.Upside: Maximum local control over what runs
options_considered.Downside: More YAML, more classifiers, and more special-case logic than the operator asked for
options_considered.Key risks: Brittle maintenance; duplicates capabilities already present in `ci.yml`

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Use a two-surface model for Brikette: `ci.yml` for changed-scope dev validation, and one Brikette publish workflow for merge-only `staging` and `main` publication with Brikette path filters and no manual dispatch.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: It matches the operator's requirements with the smallest ongoing mental model. `dev` has one job: validate changed code in CI. `staging` has one job: build and publish preview when Brikette changed. `main` has one job: build and publish live when Brikette changed. This removes the current overlap between `ci.yml`, `brikette.yml`, `brikette-staging-fast.yml`, and the reusable-app path while keeping the branch flow the repo already uses.
chosen_approach.What it depends on: Narrowing or isolating the parts of `ci.yml` that currently widen beyond Brikette's changed-scope requirement, folding staging and production publish behavior into one explicit Brikette deploy workflow, and carrying forward any production-only health checks now hidden inside the reusable-app abstraction.

rejected_approaches: Option A — rejected because it trims symptoms but leaves the same distributed contract in place. The operator would still be reasoning across three Brikette-related workflow surfaces.

Option C — rejected because custom file-level classifier logic is more complicated than the stated requirement and duplicates capabilities the repo already has through push diff plus affected-scope evaluation.

planning_focus: Define the exact changed-scope validation contract Brikette should inherit from `ci.yml`, including which wideners must be removed or bypassed for Brikette-only pushes.
Design the single Brikette publish workflow that handles both `staging` and `main` branch pushes with path filters and branch-specific build/deploy settings.
Remove Brikette dev validation and manual dispatch paths from the current app workflows.
validation_implications: Workflow validation should prove that Brikette-relevant pushes on `dev` run lint/typecheck/test only where relevant.
Branch-trigger validation should prove that `staging` and `main` publish only on Brikette-relevant merges and never via manual dispatch.
Production planning must preserve any required post-deploy health checks currently supplied by `reusable-app.yml`.
sequencing_constraints: Finalize the target workflow boundary before editing triggers, otherwise path filters and branch responsibilities will drift.
Preserve production-only healthcheck behavior before removing Brikette's reusable-app production path.
Remove manual dispatch only after the branch-triggered publish path is confirmed complete.
risks_to_carry_forward: `ci.yml` may need either targeted narrowing or a Brikette-specific entry path if repo-global governance jobs cannot be cleanly separated.
Consolidating staging and production publish into one workflow must not accidentally drop required production verification.
